## בכל דרכיך דעהו

_Section: בכל דרכיך דעהו_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="20"].

**English:**

## In All Your Ways Know Him

 The concept of "In all your ways know Him" — that one must serve Hashem not only through the performance of mitzvos, but also through one's mundane affairs — is a fundamental principle in the service of Hashem. This is not merely a secondary aspect of avodah, but rather a core component: through the refinement of the physical world, one draws down the light of Elokus into the lowest levels of reality.

Seemingly, one might ask: if the purpose of a Jew is to be occupied with Torah and mitzvos, why must one involve himself in the mundane at all? Why not remain entirely detached from the material world? 

But in truth, the purpose of the descent of the neshamah into the body and the physical world is specifically to elevate the material. By engaging in "all your ways"—the necessary activities of daily life—with the intention of serving Hashem, one performs an act of iskafya and eventually is'hapcha. This transforms the physical act itself into a vessel for Elokus, revealing that even within the mundane, there is no existence apart from Hashem. 

And this is the deeper meaning of "know Him": it is not merely an intellectual awareness, but an experiential knowledge—a dveikus—that permeates every aspect of one's life. When a Chassid conducts his business or his personal affairs with this kavanah, he is not stepping away from his avodah; he is fulfilling it in the most complete sense, bringing the light of the Torah into the very fabric of the world.

---


## בכל מאדך

_Section: בכל מאדך_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="7"].

**English:**

## בכל מאדך



The Gemara states: "‘With all your *me'odecha’ (might/wealth) — with all your wealth; and another opinion says: ‘with all your *me'odecha’ — with every measure that He measures out for you, whether it be a measure of good or a measure of [what appears to be] suffering."

On the face of it, the two interpretations of the word *me'odecha*—wealth, and the measure of one's circumstances—seem to be two distinct, unrelated matters. One refers to the *avodah* of utilizing one's material possessions for the sake of Hashem, while the other refers to the *avodah* of *bitul* and acceptance of Hashem’s will regarding the events that befall a person.

However, in truth, these two interpretations are intrinsically connected. The *avodah* of "with all your wealth" is the *preparation* and the *vessel* for the *avodah* of "with every measure that He measures out for you."

When a person realizes that his wealth is not his own, but rather a deposit entrusted to him by the Ribono shel Olam to be used for His purposes, he attains a state of *bitul*. He recognizes that he is merely a conduit. Once he has internalized this level of *bitul*—where he does not view his material possessions as "his own"—it becomes possible for him to reach the higher level of *avodah*: accepting "every measure" that Hashem decrees for him with the same equanimity. Because he has already nullified his sense of self-ownership regarding his wealth, he can now nullify his sense of self regarding his personal circumstances, recognizing that both the "good" and the "suffering" are simply different ways in which the Ribono shel Olam conducts His world.

Thus, the two interpretations are one: the *avodah* of *me'odecha* is the total surrender of one's entire existence—both that which is external to him (his wealth) and that which is internal to his experience (the measures of his life)—to the will of Hashem.

## Galus

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="52"] (In the footsteps of Moshiach, there are open miracles so that Bnei Yisrael can perform their avodah without obstacles and delays). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="147"] (Compared to the journey of Bnei Yisrael in the desert to create the Mishkan and a dwelling place in the lower worlds. [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="148"] (Tzadikim are above the matter of galus). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="173"] (Giving honor to the monarchy and the principle of dina d’malchusa dina, yet one must know that the existence of Bnei Yisrael is in the hands of the Ribono shel Olam, above nature, etc.). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="189"] (In the final generations of the first exile, the internal powers of the mind and heart were always separated from mundane activities, unlike in the final generations of the second exile). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="230"] (The specific details of the perfection of the days of Moshiach depend on the details of our actions and our avodah throughout the time of galus). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="273"] (In galus, the Ribono shel Olam ascends higher and higher).

See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="עקבתא דמשיחא"].

## דון מינה ומינה, מינה ואוקי באתרה

_Section: דון מינה ומינה, מינה ואוקי באתרה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="59"].

**English:**

## Don Minei U’minei, Minei V’oki B’asrah

 The principle of *don minei u’minei*—deriving a law from the subject itself—and *minei v’oki b’asrah*—deriving it from the subject and establishing it in its own place—is a fundamental method of Talmudic reasoning. It is not merely a technical tool for extracting a halachic ruling, but a reflection of the inner logic of the Torah itself. When the Torah presents a specific law within a particular context, that context is not incidental; it is the very source from which the law’s internal structure and application are derived. By examining the law *from* the subject itself, we uncover the essential connection between the *mitzvah* and the specific circumstances in which it is situated. This ensures that our understanding of the *halacha* is not an external imposition, but an organic outgrowth of the Torah’s own framework, firmly established in its proper place.

---

## הקזת דם

_Section: הקזת דם_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="263"].

**English:**

## Bloodletting



The Gemara states that "bloodletting is a sign of healing for some, and a sign of illness for others." This concept is also reflected in the spiritual realm, specifically regarding the *avodah* of *iskafya* (subjugation) and *is'hapcha* (transformation).

On the face of it, *iskafya*—the act of suppressing and breaking one’s base desires—is a form of "bloodletting." It involves removing the "excess blood," the vitality that fuels the *sitra achra*. For some, this is a "sign of healing," as it serves as the necessary *avodah* to purify the soul and bring it to a state of health and *bitul*. For others, however, it may be a "sign of illness," implying that their *avodah* should not be focused on the harshness of *iskafya*, but rather on the more refined *avodah* of *is'hapcha*, where the darkness itself is transformed into light.

Just as in the physical world, where the necessity of bloodletting depends on the specific condition of the patient, so too in the *avodah* of a Chossid: one must discern whether his current state requires the "healing" of *iskafya*—to cut away the *kelipah*—or whether he has reached the level where he can engage in the *avodah* of *is'hapcha*, elevating his *taanug* and *hispaalus* to *Kedushah*.

---

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="202"] (על הרב והמחנך להעמיד תלמידים הר­בה, ולהשלים עד הפרטים החיצוניים וה­אחרונים של התלמיד). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="224"] (ע"פ דרכו גו', ולאידך צ"ל למעלה מטו"ד ובמס"נ. צ"ל נצחי גם כאשר המחונך יצא מ­ביהכנ"ס וביהמ"ד כו'. עבודתנו בזה"ג הוא זמן החינוך על לעת"ל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="281"] (מרדכי גדול בדורו ולמד בעצמו עם תשב"ר. גאולתן של ישראל תלוי בחינוך דור ה­צעיר).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="גניבה"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="פורים"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="קטן"].

**English:**

## Chinuch

[ftnref_ls31_68] The obligation to answer even the question of your son "tomorrow," which refers to a time later and removed from the father’s own path. [ftnref_ls31_122] Whether it is the mitzvah of the father or the mitzvah of the son. [ftnref_ls31_202] The Rav and educator must raise many students and perfect even the student's external and final details. [ftnref_ls31_224] "According to his way," etc., yet on the other hand, it must be above intellect and emotion and with mesiras nefesh. It must be eternal, even when the one being educated has left the beis knesses and beis medrash, etc. Our avodah in this time of golus is the period of chinuch for the time of Moshiach. [ftnref_ls31_281] Mordechai was great in his generation and studied personally with school children. The redemption of Yisrael depends on the chinuch of the younger generation.

See also: geneivah, Purim, katan.

## ידיעה ובחירה

_Section: ידיעה ובחירה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="31"].

**English:**

## Knowledge and Choice



The concept of *yedi'ah* (knowledge) and *bechirah* (choice) is well known: How can man have free choice if Hashem knows in advance what he will choose? For if Hashem knows that he will choose a certain path, then he must choose it, and if he does not choose it, Hashem’s knowledge would be incorrect—which is impossible. And if he must choose it, then he has no free choice.

This is a famous question, and the Rambam writes that the answer is beyond human comprehension: "We do not have the capacity to know how the Holy One, Blessed be He, knows all the creations and their deeds." Just as we cannot comprehend the essence of Hashem, we cannot comprehend His knowledge.

However, in Chassidus, it is explained that this question arises only when we view Hashem’s knowledge as being analogous to human knowledge. Human knowledge is separate from the object being known; the object exists independently, and the person then acquires knowledge of it. Therefore, the knowledge is dependent on the object. But Hashem’s knowledge is not separate from Him—"He, His knowledge, and His life are one." His knowledge is not something that happens to Him as a result of the object; rather, the object comes into existence *because* of His knowledge.

Thus, the fact that Hashem knows how a person will choose does not force the person to choose that way. On the contrary, the person’s choice is the very thing that Hashem knows. Hashem’s knowledge encompasses the future just as it encompasses the present, and He knows that the person will choose freely. The choice remains entirely in the hands of the person, and that choice is what is known to Hashem.

---

In the Beis HaMikdash, they would pronounce the Name as it is written. The Havayah of Above and the Havayah of below. In the time to come, these two names of Havayah will be one.

## כיסוי ראש

_Section: כיסוי ראש_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="270"] (במנהג דכיפה תחת הכובע).

**English:**

## Regarding the Custom of Wearing a Kippah Under the Hat

The custom of wearing a *kippah* under the hat is a practice that has been observed by many, and it is rooted in the principle of *yiras shamayim*. 

On the face of it, one might ask: if the head is already covered by a hat, why is there a need for an additional covering? Yet, in truth, this practice reflects a deeper *avodah*. Just as the *neshamah* is clothed in various levels—*nefesh, ru'ach,* and *neshamah*—so too, the external expression of one's *yirah* should be layered. The hat represents the outer covering, while the *kippah* serves as the immediate, constant reminder of the *Ribono shel Olam* who stands above one's head. 

By maintaining this double covering, a *Chassid* internalizes the awareness that even beneath the formal attire required for public conduct, there remains a private, constant state of *bitul* and *yirah* before Hashem. This is not merely a matter of custom, but a tangible expression of the *avodah* to ensure that one's *dveikus* is not only for the public eye, but is deeply rooted in one's personal, private reality.

---

## כשרות האכו&quot;ש

_Section: כשרות האכו&quot;ש_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="229"].

**English:**

## The Kashrus of Non-Jewish Food



The Gemara states that the prohibition against eating food cooked by a non-Jew (*bishul akum*) was enacted for a specific reason: "lest he come to marry her." This is a decree of the Sages, intended to prevent social closeness that could lead to intermarriage.

However, we must understand why this specific prohibition—the cooking of food—is singled out as a cause for concern regarding intermarriage. After all, there are many other forms of social interaction that could potentially lead to such a result. Why is it specifically the act of cooking that the Sages identified as a catalyst for this danger?

The answer lies in the nature of the act of cooking. Cooking is not merely a technical preparation of food; it is a process that creates a bond between the one who prepares the food and the one who consumes it. When a person prepares a meal for another, they are investing their own effort and care into the sustenance of the other. This creates a sense of intimacy and familiarity. 

In the context of *avodah*, we see that the physical act of eating is a means of elevating the sparks of *Elokus* contained within the food. When a Jew eats, they are performing a *mitzvah*—provided the food is kosher and prepared in a manner consistent with the holiness of a Jewish home. When the cooking is done by a non-Jew, this process of elevation is compromised. The *bitul* and *kavanah* that should accompany the preparation of food are absent, and instead, the food becomes a vehicle for a connection that is outside the framework of *kedushah*.

Thus, the Sages decreed that the act of cooking by a non-Jew is forbidden, because it fosters a level of social closeness that bypasses the boundaries established by the Torah to maintain the distinct identity of the Jewish people. By restricting this, the Sages ensured that the home remains a place of *kedushah*, where the bonds formed are rooted in shared *avodah* and commitment to the *mitzvos*.

---

[ftnref_ls31_71] Includes the entire generation, and through him, the unity of the entire generation. "From his shoulders and upward" (i.e., he is superior to the rest).

[ftnref_ls31_75] The influence of the Nasi of the generation upon the people of the generation, and how his influence is distinct from that of other leaders and tzaddikim of the generation. The general yechidah of the generation.

[ftnref_ls31_147] The Nesi'im of Israel are called "cedars" (*arazim*), a term denoting elevation (*hitnas'ut*).

[ftnref_ls31_148] The acronym (*roshei teivos*) for Nasi: "The spark of Yaakov Avinu" (*Nitzotzo shel Yaakov Avinu*). A general soul that unites all of Israel.

## עקבתא דמשיחא

_Section: עקבתא דמשיחא_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="98"] (דור האחרון דגלות הוא גלגול דור יוצאי מצרים). **English:**

## עקבתא דמשיחא

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="98"] (The final generation of the galus is a reincarnation of the generation that left Mitzrayim). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="202"] (The virtue of the avodah in our generation, in the completion of the birurim and in the "heels" [of Mashiach], is that it is the one that finishes and brings the Geulah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="224"] (In the generation of the ikvesa d'Meshicha, "there is no one among us who knows how long" [Tehillim 74:9] regarding the secret of the avodah of birurim. The avodah is in the manner of "snatch and eat," etc.).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="גלות"].

## The Akeidah

## Obstinacy

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="31"] (Stiff-necked of the *kelipah*). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="32"]-[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="31"] (Of *kelipah*, and of *kedushah*).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="פרעה"] (Pharaoh).

ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="משיח"].

## Rich and Poor

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="216"] (Whether there were rich and poor people at the time of the contributions for the Mishkan. The virtue of the poor person’s offering).

(This applies only to coarse matter; it is otherwise when it has been refined.)

## The Ten Commandments

## פורים

_Section: פורים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="170"] (במארז"ל בטעם הגזירה מפני שנהנו מסעודתו של אותו רשע). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="174"] (נס המלו­בש בטבע). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="177"] (חייב איניש לבסומי כו' והסיפור דרבה ור' זירא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="231"] (הבדל בהיחס דמרדכי ואסתר להגזירה. אסתר פרסה נדה כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="77"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="276"] (ורבים מעמי ה­ארץ מתייהדים. ונהפוך הוא גו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="278"] (כל המועדים בטלים חוץ מפורים. בדין ד­כפרים מקדימים ליום הכניסה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="279"] (ע"ד כפילת ושינוי אמירת "ולהרוג" "לפני­הם". ברכת הרב ריבנו ביחיד). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="280"] (זהי­רות בחינוך ילדים, שבהצגת פורים לא יתחפשו וישחקו בתפקיד "המן הרשע").
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="אסתר"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" displayterm="זכור" goterm="זכור, פ' זכור"].

**English:**

## Purim

[ftnref_ls31_170] Regarding the Sages' explanation for the decree, that it was because they derived pleasure from the feast of that wicked one. [ftnref_ls31_174] A miracle clothed in nature. [ftnref_ls31_177] "A person is obligated to become intoxicated, etc.," and the story of Rabbah and Rabbi Zeira. [ftnref_ls31_231] The difference in the approach of Mordechai and Esther to the decree; Esther's menstruation, etc. [ftnref_ls31_77]-[ftnref_ls31_276] "And many of the people of the land became Jews; and it was turned around, etc." [ftnref_ls31_278] All festivals will be nullified except for Purim; the law regarding the villages that precede the day of gathering. [ftnref_ls31_279] Regarding the repetition and variation in the phrasing of "to kill" and "before them"; the blessing of the Rav, Ribono, in private. [ftnref_ls31_280] Caution in the education of children, that in Purim plays they should not dress up or act out the role of "Haman the wicked."

See also: Esther, Zachor (Parshas Zachor).

---

_Section: פנים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="189"] (מורה על כוחות הפנימיים. טהרת הפנים לפני התפילה).

**English:**

## Pnim

(Refers to the internal powers. The purification of the *pnim* before *tefillah*.)

---


## פנימיות התורה

_Section: פנימיות התורה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="115"] (כל הלכה שבתורה יש לה גם פירוש בעבודה רוחנית). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="179"] (שתויי יין — גודל ועוצם הגילוי דפנימיה"ת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="182"] (ע"ד הנ"ל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="69"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="268"] (לימוד פנימיה"ת סגולה ותרופה למאור העיניים).

**English:**

## פנימיות התורה

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="115"] (Every halacha in the Torah also has an interpretation in spiritual avodah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="179"] (Those intoxicated by wine — the greatness and intensity of the revelation of Pnimiyus HaTorah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="182"] (On the aforementioned matter). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="69"]-[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="268"] (The study of Pnimiyus HaTorah is a segulah and a remedy for the illumination of the eyes).

---


## פסח

_Section: פסח_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="53"] (דין חגיגה הבאה עם הפסח). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="55"] (גדרים במצות הסיבה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="59"] (סיפור יצי"מ יסוד גדול ועמוד חזק כו'. ב' הענינים: ר"ה לרגלים, וחה"פ נמשך תמיד). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="61"] (ב­שאלת הבן, כי ישאלך בנך מחר גו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="66"] (בהגדה: כנגד ד' בנים דיברה תורה כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="281"] (בליל הסדר: עשיית כמה דברים שלא יישנו התינוקות, הוראה לחינוך).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="מצה"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="קרבן פסח"].

**English:**

## Pesach

 The law regarding the Chagigah offering brought with the Pesach sacrifice. Parameters of the mitzvah of reclining (heseibah). The recounting of the Exodus from Egypt as a fundamental principle and sturdy pillar, etc. Two aspects: Rosh Hashanah for the festivals, and the fact that the Festival of Pesach is always ongoing. Regarding the son’s question: "When your son asks you tomorrow, etc." In the Haggadah: "The Torah speaks regarding four sons, etc." On the night of the Seder: Performing various actions so that the children do not fall asleep, a lesson in chinuch.

See also: Matzah, Korban Pesach.

---


## פקוח נפש

_Section: פקוח נפש_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="254"] (דוחה כה"ת).

**English:**

## Pikuach Nefesh

 (Overrides the entire Torah).

---


## פרה

_Section: פרה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="284"] (בענין שאין להפסיק בין פרשת פרה לפרשת החודש).

**English:**

## Regarding the requirement not to interrupt between Parshas Parah and Parshas HaChodesh

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="284"] (Regarding the matter of not interrupting between Parshas Parah and Parshas HaChodesh).

---


## פרנסה

_Section: פרנסה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="173"] (ברכת ה' היא תעשיר, ומ"מ צריך עשיית כלי. פרנסה בזה"ז היא כמו השפ­עת מן). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="189"] (צ"ל יגיע כפיך ולא יגיע המוח ולב).

**English:**

## Parnassah

The blessing of Hashem is what brings wealth, yet one must still create a vessel. Parnassah in our current time is like the influence of the manna. One must exert the labor of one's hands, but not the labor of the mind and heart.

---


## פרעה

_Section: פרעה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="31"] (אותיות הערף, עקשות דקליפה).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="מצרים"].

**English:**

## Pharaoh

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="31"] (The letters of *oref* [neck/stiffness], the stubbornness of *kelipah*).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="מצרים"] (Mitzrayim).

_Section: צבת_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="106"] (צבת בצבת עשוי').

**English:**

## Tzeves

(Tzeves b'tzeves asui — "Tongs are made with tongs.")

---


## צדיקים

_Section: צדיקים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="74"] (דביקות בת"ח וראשי בנ"י שבדורם). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="147"] (נקראים ארזים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="148"] (הם למע' מ­ענין הגלות).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="נשיא"].

**English:**

## Tzadikim

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="74"] (Dveikus with Talmidei Chachamim and the heads of Bnei Yisrael in their generation). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="147"] (They are called cedars). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="148"] (They are above the concept of Galus).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="נשיא"] (Nasi).

---


## צדקה

_Section: צדקה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="7"] (די מחסורו, לעשרו. למע' מטבעו ורגי­לותו). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="117"] (הנותן סלע לעני ע"מ שיחי' בני). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="239"] (כל הבית צ"ל ממולא מצדקה וגמ"ח. גמ"ח בנפש גוף וממון). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="241"] (כל המצות נק' בשם צדקה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="43"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="242"] (לכל ילד צ"ל קופת צדקה משלו). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="256"] (גדול ה­מעשה יותר מן העושה).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="נרות שבת קודש"].

**English:**

## Tzedakah

 (His lack, to enrich him. Beyond his nature and habit). (One who gives a sela to a poor person so that his son may live). (The entire home must be filled with tzedakah and gemilas chasadim. Gemilas chasadim in soul, body, and money). (All mitzvos are called by the name tzedakah). - (Every child must have a tzedakah pushka of their own). (The act is greater than the one who performs it).

See also [ftnref_goterm_1] (Shabbos Kodesh candles).

---


## צורה וחומר

_Section: צורה וחומר_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="139"] (כל דבר שבעולם נחלק לג' חלקים: עצם הדבר, תכונת הדבר שבזה חלוק מ­זולת, ושאר פרטי כל דבר שאינם חלק מתכונתו. צורה וחומר נבראו מרצון ומ­ דיבור).

**English:**

## Form and Matter

Everything in the world is divided into three parts: the essence of the thing; the specific quality of the thing by which it is distinguished from others; and the remaining details of the thing that are not part of its essential quality. Form and matter were created through Will and Speech.

---


## קבלת עול

_Section: קבלת עול_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="32"] (מעלת העבודה בקב"ע). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="33"] (ב' דר­גות: למטה מכוחות הנפש, למעלה מ­כוחות הנפש).

**English:**

## Kabbalas Ol

 The virtue of avodah through Kabbalas Ol. Two levels: that which is below the kochos hanefesh, and that which is above the kochos hanefesh.

---


## קטן

_Section: קטן_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="121"] (אסרו כמה ענינים לקטן גם בלא הגיע לחינוך משום דילמא אתי למיסרך כשיגדיל. גם בלא הגיע לחינוך הוא בגדר תומ"צ). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="43"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="242"] (לעשות מהחדר שלהם בית של תורה תפלה וגמ"ח. לכ"א צ"ל סידור חומש וקופת צדקה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="281"] (הם הכי­רוהו תחילה).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="עיר הנדחת"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="פורים"].

**English:**

## Katan

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="121"] Certain matters are forbidden to a katan even before he reaches the age of chinuch, lest he come to be accustomed to them when he grows up. Even before reaching the age of chinuch, he is within the category of one who performs Torah and mitzvos. [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="43"]-[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="242"] To make their room a house of Torah, tefillah, and gemilas chasadim. Every person must have a Siddur, a Chumash, and a tzedakah box. [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="281"] They recognized Him first.

See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="עיר הנדחת"]. [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="פורים"].

---


## קידוש

_Section: קידוש_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="259"] (יין של קידוש והבדלה סגולה ל­ מאור עיניים).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" displayterm="נש"ק" goterm="נרות שבת קודש"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="שבת"].

**English:**

## Kiddush

[ftnref_ls31_259] (The wine used for Kiddush and Havdalah is a *segulah* for the illumination of the eyes).
See also: *neshek* (Shabbos candles). Shabbos.

---


## קידוש ידים ורגלים

_Section: קידוש ידים ורגלים_

**Hebrew:**

ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="כהנים"].

**English:**

## קידוש ידים ורגלים

See [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="כהנים"].

---


## קרבן פסח

_Section: קרבן פסח_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="57"] (בדין צולין אותו שלם. אם דין צלייתו קשור עם אכילתו). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="58"] (דין אכילתו על השובע). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="232"] (שייכותו למילה).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="פסח"].

**English:**

## Korban Pesach

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="57"] (Regarding the law of roasting it whole. Whether the law of its roasting is connected to the law of its eating). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="58"] (The law of eating it while satiated). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="232"] (Its connection to milah).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls31" goterm="פסח"].

---


## קרבנות

_Section: קרבנות_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="134"] (נק' עבודה סתם ורומזת על כללות העבודה והקירוב לה'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="223"] (מקריבין ה­קרבנות אע"פ שאין שם בית בנוי). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="238"] (אדם כי יקריב מכם. הקרבת חלבו ודמו. מלשון קירוב).

**English:**

## Korbanos

(The term *avodah* in a general sense, alluding to the totality of one’s *avodah* and the drawing close to Hashem.)

(We offer *korbanos* even when the Beis Hamikdash is not built.)

("When a man among you brings an offering" (*Adam ki yakriv mikem*). The offering of one’s fat and blood. From the expression of *kiruv* — drawing close.)

---


## קריעת ים סוף

_Section: קריעת ים סוף_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="69"] (אופן אמירת השירה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="70"] (אחדות ישראל בעת אמירת השירה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="80"] (בטעם שנכלל בשירת הים גם ע"ד הכניסה לא"י ובנין ביהמ"ק). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="82"] (שירת הים פתיחה ל­כל השירות). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="235"] (בהמובא בספרים שגם אם הי' בטבע, הנס הוא מה שהקב"ה הט­ ביע בתחילת הבריאה שביום פלוני יבקע הים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="281"] (הקטנים הכירוהו תחילה).

**English:**

## The Splitting of the Sea

[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="69"] (The manner in which the song was recited). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="70"] (The unity of Yisrael at the time of the song's recitation). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="80"] (The reason why the Song of the Sea also includes references to the entry into Eretz Yisrael and the building of the Beis Hamikdash). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="82"] (The Song of the Sea as the opening for all songs). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="235"] (Regarding the concept brought in the sefarim that even if it occurred within nature, the miracle lies in the fact that Hashem embedded it at the beginning of creation—that on a specific day, the sea would split). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="281"] (The young children recognized Him first).

---


## קשת

_Section: קשת_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="245"] (אות ברית הוא, אף שמבואר שזהו"ע בטבע).

**English:**

## Keshes

(It is a sign of the covenant, even though it is explained that this is a matter of nature.)

---


## ראי'

_Section: ראי'_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="249"] (עד ט"ז מיל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="271"] (צ"ל סיגופי נה"ב בהגדרת חוש הראי' כו').
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="רפואה"].

**English:**

## Proof

[ftnref_ls31_249] (up to sixteen mil). [ftnref_ls31_271] (it should say: the afflictions of the animal soul in the definition of the sense of sight, etc.).
See also [ftnref_ls31_refuah] (healing).

---


## ראש השנה

_Section: ראש השנה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="33"] (חילוק קב"ע דר"ה לקב"ע דכל ה­שנה).

**English:**

## Rosh Hashanah

(The distinction between the *kabalas ol* of Rosh Hashanah and the *kabalas ol* of the rest of the year.)

---


## רב ותלמיד

_Section: רב ותלמיד_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="95"] (לעולם ישנה אדם לתלמידו בדרך קצרה).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="חינוך"].

## Rav and Talmid

A person should always teach his student in a concise manner. 
See also: Chinuch.

## Rabbah

 (Regarding the story where Rabbah arose and slaughtered R. Zeira, etc.). (His name indicates greatness).

## רפואה

_Section: רפואה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="246"] (כי אני ה' רופאך ר"ת אריך, כי כל אסוותא מלעילא. לא אשים עליך מעיקרא כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="262"] (שימוש ברפואות המונעים מ­מחלות. זריקות גם מדברים בלתי כשרים. דקדוק בעירוי דם מי הוא הנותן הדם). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="263"] (בענין הקזה. שלילות דאגה ומרה שחורה בעניני רפואה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="264"] (ניתנה רשות לרופא לרפאות. צריך לחזק התקוה וה­בטחון. להחזיק בחדרו בבית הרפואה מזוזה כשרה, וקבלת צדקה על עצמו. ע"ד ניתוח בהשקדים בגרון). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="265"] (אמירת כל התהלים בש"ק לפני ניתוח. על החולה לא להתערב בעניני הרופאים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="266"] (לחזק ה­בטחון). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="267"] (לפעמים הוא ע"פ נס. רשות לרופא לרפאות. יוסיף דעת יוסיף מ­כאוב). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="268"] (סיפור על א' שאדה"ז ריפא אותו בחתיכת מצה שמורה וחצי כוס מים — בלבוש דטבע. הלחש למי שנתחב לו עצם כו', שהיו למדים אותו אפילו לתינו­קות של בית הרב). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="70"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="268"] (סגולה ותרו­פה למאור העיניים: לימוד פנימיה"ת, זהירות בקידוש והבדלה, השתתפות ב­נרות דביהכ"נ).

**English:**

## Refuah

[ftnref_ls31_246] "For I am Hashem your healer" (*Ki Ani Hashem Rofecha*)—the initials spell *Arich*; for all healing comes from above. "I will not place upon you from the beginning, etc."

[ftnref_ls31_262] The use of medicines that prevent illness. Injections, even those derived from non-kosher substances. Scrupulousness regarding blood transfusions: who is the donor of the blood?

[ftnref_ls31_263] Regarding bloodletting. The negation of worry and melancholy (*marah shechorah*) in matters of health.

[ftnref_ls31_264] Permission has been granted to the physician to heal. One must strengthen hope and *bitachon*. One should keep a kosher mezuzah in one’s hospital room and undertake a commitment to *tzedakah*. Regarding a tonsillectomy.

[ftnref_ls31_265] Reciting the entire Tehillim on Shabbos before a surgery. The patient should not interfere in the physicians' decisions.

[ftnref_ls31_266] Strengthening *bitachon*.

[ftnref_ls31_267] Sometimes it is through a miracle. Permission has been granted to the physician to heal. "He who increases knowledge increases pain."

[ftnref_ls31_268] A story about an individual whom the Alter Rebbe healed with a piece of *matzah shemurah* and half a cup of water—clothed in the garments of nature. The incantation for one who has a bone stuck in their throat, etc., which they would teach even to the children of the *beis harav*.

[ftnref_ls31_70]-[ftnref_ls31_268] A *segulah* and remedy for eyesight: the study of *Pnimiyus HaTorah*, being careful with *Kiddush* and *Havdalah*, and participating in the synagogue candles.

---

## רשע

_Section: רשע_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="1"] (המגביה ידו על חבירו נק' רשע). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="7"] (רשע דקדושה — בכל אשר יפנה יצליח, ניצוח והצלחה למע' ממדידה והגבלה).

**English:**

## Rasha

(One who raises his hand against his fellow is called a *rasha*.) (A *rasha* of *kedushah* — in all that he turns to, he succeeds; victory and success that transcend measurement and limitation.)

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="235"] (שייכותו לכ"ק אדמו"ר מהוריי"צ נ"ע).
יו"ד שבט
[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="234"] (עלי' בכל שנה וגופא בתר רישא אזיל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="245"].
ט"ו בשבט
[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="235"] (ענינו ומנהגיו). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="242"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="252"] (נטיעת עצים בט"ו בשבט אין לערב זה במנהגי השו"ע כו').

כ"ב שבט
[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="93"].

**English:**

## Shevat, Month

10 Shevat
[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="235"] (Its connection to the Rebbe Rayatz).
[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="234"] (His ascent each year, and the body follows the head). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="245"].

15 Shevat
[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="235"] (Its significance and customs). [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="242"]. [mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="252"] (Planting trees on 15 Shevat; one should not conflate this with the customs of the Shulchan Aruch, etc.).

22 Shevat
[mafteach_gopage file="ls31" gopage="93"].

## שבת

_Section: שבת_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="87"] (פרשת שבת ניתנה לישראל במרה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="91"] (בטעם שניתנה לישראל דוקא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="99"] (בה­דיעה שהבדלה היא מה"ת. זכור ושמור). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="100"] (גדר מ"ע דזכור את יוה"ש לקדשו). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="101"] (אם נשים חייבות בהבדלה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="103"] (הבדלה מבעוד יום). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="104"] (הבדלה ענינה להמשיך קדושת השבת בימי החול של­אח"ז. תוספת שבת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="107"] (הלכות שבת כהררין התלויין בשערה כו'. שבות דרבנן ודאורייתא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="108"] (דין מי שהחשיך חוץ לתחום ודין קושרין קשירת נימא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="109"] (שבות דרבנן ודאורייתא. הלכות שבת כהררין כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="110"] (איסור תחומין יש לו עיקר מה"ת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="111"] (ענינה העדר ההוצאה מרשות לרשות ולגלות שכל העולם הוא ברשותו של הקב"ה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="119"] (בדין וינפש בן אמתך והגר). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="123"] (בדין שביתת בהמה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="191"] (מוצ"ש: נטילת הנשמה יתירה, וריח הבשמים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="192"] (אכילת דגים בשבת אין לו טעם בדרך הנגלה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="194"] (בשבת העיקר מנוחה, ביו"ט ביטול מלאכה. המנוחה והעונג דשבת פועלים שינוי בהגברא עד שאין דומה אור פניו של אדם כו' וגם שינוי בדפק שביד כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="198"] (כשמבדיל ביום ראשון אם גם אז יכול לברך על ה­בשמים. כשאין לו יין להבדיל במוצ"ש אם צריך לברך על הבשמים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="247"] (סדר ה­סעודות: חקל תפוחין קדישין, ע"ק ז"א. אודות הזהירות שלא לקדש משעה [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="7"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="6"] בע"ש ואם חוששין לזה באה"ק. אם אכי­לת שבת מגשם). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="248"] (שלילת תענית יארצייט בשבת. נצטוו במרה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="252"] (אילו היו ישראל משמרין כו' בן דוד בא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="257"] (קדירה ישנה רקנית על האש). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="59"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="257"] (בענין מלאכת תפירה והשתמשות במחט בטחון). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="260"] (בענין שבת זייגער. שלילת ממון הקשור עם חילול שבת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="261"] (נתינת צדקה כמספר שבת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="284"] (הבערה לחלק או ללאו יצאת).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" displayterm="יו"ט" goterm="יום טוב"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="מן"]. [mafteach\_goterm file="ls31" goterm="נרות שבת קודש"].

**English:**

## Shabbos

[ftnref_ls31_87] The parsha of Shabbos was given to the Jewish people at Marah. [ftnref_ls31_91] On the reason it was given specifically to the Jewish people. [ftnref_ls31_99] On the opinion that Havdalah is a Torah obligation. Zachor and Shamor. [ftnref_ls31_100] The parameters of the positive mitzvah to "Remember the Shabbos day to sanctify it." [ftnref_ls31_101] Whether women are obligated in Havdalah. [ftnref_ls31_103] Performing Havdalah while it is still day. [ftnref_ls31_104] The purpose of Havdalah is to draw the holiness of Shabbos into the subsequent weekdays. Tosefes Shabbos. [ftnref_ls31_107] The laws of Shabbos are like "mountains hanging by a hair," etc. Rabbinic versus Torah prohibitions. [ftnref_ls31_108] The law regarding one who becomes ben hashmashos outside the techum, and the law regarding tying a knot of a hair, etc. [ftnref_ls31_109] Rabbinic versus Torah prohibitions. The laws of Shabbos are like mountains, etc. [ftnref_ls31_110] The prohibition of techumin has a basis in the Torah. [ftnref_ls31_111] Its purpose is the absence of transferring from one domain to another, and to reveal that the entire world is in the domain of the Ribono shel Olam. [ftnref_ls31_119] On the law of "that your manservant and the stranger may rest." [ftnref_ls31_123] On the law of the rest of an animal. [ftnref_ls31_191] Motzaei Shabbos: the departure of the neshamah yeseirah, and the scent of the besamim. [ftnref_ls31_192] Eating fish on Shabbos has no rationale in the revealed dimension of Torah. [ftnref_ls31_194] On Shabbos, the essence is rest; on Yom Tov, the essence is the cessation of melachah. The rest and oneg of Shabbos effect a change in the person, to the point that "a person’s facial radiance is not the same," etc., and also a change in the pulse of the hand, etc. [ftnref_ls31_198] When making Havdalah on Sunday, whether one can still recite the blessing over the besamim. When one does not have wine for Havdalah on Motzaei Shabbos, whether one must recite the blessing over the besamim. [ftnref_ls31_247] The order of the meals: the "Field of Holy Apples," the secret of Ze'er Anpin. Regarding the caution not to make Kiddush from the hour [ftnref_ls31_7]-[ftnref_ls31_6] on Erev Shabbos, and whether this is a concern in Eretz Yisrael. Whether eating on Shabbos is physical. [ftnref_ls31_248] The negation of a yahrtzeit fast on Shabbos. They were commanded at Marah. [ftnref_ls31_252] "If the Jewish people would keep [two Shabbosos]," etc., "the son of Dovid will come." [ftnref_ls31_257] An old, empty pot on the fire. [ftnref_ls31_59]-[ftnref_ls31_257] Regarding the melachah of sewing and the use of a safety pin. [ftnref_ls31_260] Regarding a "Shabbos clock." The negation of money associated with the desecration of Shabbos. [ftnref_ls31_261] Giving tzedakah in the amount of "Shabbos." [ftnref_ls31_284] Whether the melachah of kindling (hav'arah) was singled out to be a category or a negative prohibition.

See also: Yom Tov, Man, Shabbos candles.

---

**English:**

## Shulchan

## שומרים

_Section: שומרים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="112"] (בסוגיא דארבעה שומרים הן כו'. ב­דין שוכר כשומר חנם או כשומר שכר). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="115"] (ענין הד' שומרים בעבודה הרוחנית). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="116"] (ד' שומרים לנגד ד' אותיות שם הוי'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="117"] (גדר חיובם מחמת שיעבוד השומר או מחמת גזיה"כ. בדין שוכר כנ"ל).

**English:**

## Guardians

 Regarding the sugya of the four types of guardians, etc. (concerning the law of whether a renter is considered a gratuitous guardian or a paid guardian). The concept of the four guardians in spiritual avodah. The four guardians corresponding to the four letters of the name Havayah. The definition of their obligation—whether it stems from the commitment of the guardian or from a specific scriptural decree (gezeiras hakasuv). Regarding the law of a renter, as mentioned above.

---

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="277"] (אשר ישלטו גו' בשונאיהם, אתהפכא דשונאיהם מחשוכא לנהורא).

## Shonei

(That they shall rule over their enemies, the *is'hapcha* of their enemies from darkness to light.)

_Section: שחיטה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="181"] (ענינה העלאת הנשחט, אין ושחט אלא ומשך).

## Shchitah

 (Its concept is the elevation of that which is slaughtered, for "slaughtering" [shchitah] refers only to "drawing" [meshech]).

## שינויים

_Section: שינויים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="26"] (אני ה' לא שניתי. מעלת העבודה בלי שינוי, שהן בתורה הן בתפילה או במצות מאיר אצלו אותו נקודת הביטול). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="32"] (עבודה בקבלת עול שהוא רחוק משי­נויים).

**English:**

## Changes

"I, Hashem, have not changed" (Malachi 3:6). The virtue of avodah that is without change—whether in Torah, in tefillah, or in mitzvos—is that the same point of bitul shines within it.

Avodah through kabbalas ol, which is far removed from changes.

## שלמה, רש&quot;י

_Section: שלמה, רש&quot;י_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="28"] (לשון "תרגומו" בפירש"י עה"ת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="34"] (לפעמים גם תיבות שבתוך פירושו הם חלק מהד"ה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="62"] (גם כשמשתמש בלשון חז"ל, כוונתו למשמעות הפשוטה של לשון וביטוי זה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="80"] (שיטתו בהכפלת ל­שונות בשירות שבכתובים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="128"] (צ"ע אם חלוקת פרשיות שבס"ת שייכת ללימוד פשש"מ). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="137"] (אין ענינו להעתיק ולפרש את דברי התרגום). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="143"] (דרך ציונו להמד­רשים שהם מקור דבריו). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="165"] (רש"י כותב פעמים רבות שהכתוב הוא מקרא קצר). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="204"] (לפעמים התיבות שבתוך פי­רושו הם חלק מהד"ה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="215"] (לשון רש"י: "לקיים מה שנאמר כו'". שינויים בלשו­נות הכתובים בין ציווי ה' למשה לדברי משה לבנ"י).

**English:**

## Shlomo, Rashi

[ftnref_ls31_28] The language of "his translation" in Rashi’s commentary on the Torah. [ftnref_ls31_34] Sometimes, the words within his commentary are part of the *dibbur hamaschil*. [ftnref_ls31_62] Even when he uses the language of Chazal, his intent is the simple meaning of that expression and phrase. [ftnref_ls31_80] His methodology regarding the repetition of terms in the songs found in Scripture. [ftnref_ls31_128] It requires further study whether the division of *parshiyos* in a Sefer Torah is relevant to the study of *peshuto shel mikra*. [ftnref_ls31_137] His purpose is not to copy and explain the words of the Targum. [ftnref_ls31_143] His method of citing the Midrashim that serve as the source for his words. [ftnref_ls31_165] Rashi writes many times that the verse is a *mikra katzar* (an abbreviated text). [ftnref_ls31_204] Sometimes, the words within his commentary are part of the *dibbur hamaschil*. [ftnref_ls31_215] Rashi’s language: "To fulfill that which is said, etc." Changes in the language of the verses between Hashem’s command to Moshe and Moshe’s words to the Bnei Yisrael.

## תורה שבכתב

_Section: תורה שבכתב_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls31" gopage="283"] (בענין לימוד תושב"כ בעל­פה).

**English:**

## Regarding the study of Torah Shebichtav by heart

The requirement to study Torah Shebichtav by heart is a matter that requires clarification. Seemingly, the prohibition against reciting Torah Shebichtav by heart—as stated in the Gemara (Gittin 60b), "Words that were written, you are not permitted to say by heart"—would apply even when one is studying or reviewing it. 

Yet, we find that the halacha permits, and even encourages, the memorization of Torah Shebichtav. This is based on the principle that the prohibition is specifically regarding the act of reading from memory for the purpose of teaching or public recitation, whereas the internalizing of Torah through study is a fundamental aspect of *avodah*. 

When a person engages in the study of Torah Shebichtav by heart, they are not merely reciting words; they are internalizing the *Elokus* contained within the text. This process of *hisbonenus* allows the Torah to become part of the *neshamah* itself. Thus, the study of Torah Shebichtav by heart is not a violation of the prohibition, but rather a higher level of *dveikus*, where the *neshamah* and the Torah become one. 

Furthermore, this practice is essential for the *chossid* in their daily *avodah*. By carrying the words of Torah Shebichtav within their heart, the *chossid* ensures that their thoughts, speech, and actions are constantly aligned with the will of Hashem, reflecting the ultimate goal of *teshuvah* and the refinement of the world.
